sources.claim: Scripts.txt contains semicolon-delimited code point ranges and Script values; unlisted code points default to Unknown. ScriptExtensions.txt contains short Script aliases, overrides the default, and otherwise defaults to the singleton Script value.

finding: Restriction-level classification should consume exact-version Script_Extensions sets derived from authenticated Unicode data. Explicit ScriptExtensions.txt entries override the singleton Script fallback; absent Script records resolve to Unknown.
design_implication: Insert a fail-closed parser and binary-search lookup layer between authenticated Unicode bundle verification and restriction-level classification. Preserve file authentication, alias normalization, profile eligibility, confusable checks, and bidi safety as separate responsibilities.

verification.scope: byte-identical pre-commit files in isolated Node runtime

uncertainty: The parser has not yet been run against the complete Unicode 17.0.0 data files in repository CI.
Scripts.txt long names and ScriptExtensions.txt four-letter aliases require an authenticated alias-normalization layer before direct use by the existing restriction classifier.
Data byte authentication and exact-version digest verification remain upstream requirements.
falsification_route: Provide a valid Unicode data record rejected by the parser.
Provide overlapping or surrogate-containing ranges accepted by the parser.
Provide an explicit ScriptExtensions.txt entry that fails to override the Script fallback.
Run the complete Unicode 17.0.0 files and compare sampled and boundary lookups with a trusted independent implementation.
